content: Just as the linguistic definition of reflexion frames it as both the physical reflection of light and the deliberate cognitive act of reflecting on experience, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to serve this dual function: not only reflecting the model’s existing problem-solving experience back to it, but also acting as a 'cognitive mirror' that reflects external linguistic biases and unstated assumptions encoded in the model’s training data. This dual framing turns self-reflection from a mere performance optimization tool into a mechanism for active debiasing.
